title: QUIC Transport Bridge, Diagnostics, and Sender/Recovery Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the new library seam classes.
expected_result: The TLS bridge state can represent transport-parameter, key-lifecycle, handshake-confirmation, and fatal-alert facts; the diagnostics sink is cheap when disabled; and the sender/recovery owner retains packet-sent state and PTO ownership in the library.
procedure: Run the new CRT requirement-home tests covering the TLS bridge state, diagnostics sink, and sender/recovery runtime owner.
Inspect the seam implementation files to confirm they remain library-owned and do not embed interop-runner-specific behavior.
Confirm the solution build and test pass include the new seam files.
scope: This slice verifies the library-owned transport/runtime seams that future hosts and the interop harness will reuse. It does not prove end-to-end QUIC transport behavior.
preconditions: The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.
